evidence: BlackLabSnippetParser.java houses ~8 @Deprecated methods (extractCollocateFromSnippet, extractCollocateFromMatch, extractCollocateFromPlainText, extractTokenFromSnippet, etc.) alongside the clean consolidated API (extractLemmaAt, extractHeadLemma, extractCollocateLemma) in the same file — deprecated methods are still present and accessible, creating a two-API island within one class

SemanticFieldExplorer.java contains 6 inner result classes (ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, ExploreOptions/record, Edge) that are imported and used from WordSketchApiServer.java — the class serves as both algorithm and model package, diverging from the flat-class convention used everywhere else

WordSketchApiServer.java is public; SketchHandlers.java is package-private — two classes in the api/ package that both contain HTTP handler methods follow different access modifiers, creating an asymmetric organizational pattern for sibling handlers

WordSketchApiServer delegates some handler logic internally (handleSketchRequest, handleFullSketch, etc.) while SketchHandlers contains other handlers — the boundary between which handlers live in which class is not self-evident

CLAUDE.md references a grammar/ package containing CQLParser, CQLPattern, CQLParseException, but that package does not exist in the codebase — the documentation is stale and describes a prior organization that was refactored away
All method and field names use camelCase; all class names use PascalCase; all constants use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE — the core Java naming conventions are applied uniformly throughout
Package structure (api, config, indexer, query, tagging, utils, viz) is logical and consistently scoped — no style islands at the package level

strengths: Java naming conventions (camelCase, PascalCase, UPPER_SNAKE_CASE) are applied uniformly across all production files
Package organization is clear and consistently scoped — api, config, query, utils, viz each contain cohesive classes with no cross-package outliers
The utils/ package correctly aggregates shared primitives (CqlUtils, LogDiceCalculator, LongIntHashMap, PosGroup) that would otherwise be duplicated
convention_outlier.issue_character: The outlier pattern is concentrated accumulation: deprecated methods not removed from BlackLabSnippetParser, inner model classes not extracted from SemanticFieldExplorer, and split handler responsibility between WordSketchApiServer and SketchHandlers without a clear organizing principle.
convention_outlier.score_rationale: Core naming and package conventions are consistently applied, which prevents this from scoring lower. However, the presence of a deprecated-method island in BlackLabSnippetParser, a model-class island inside SemanticFieldExplorer (acknowledged but unresolved technical debt), and the ambiguous split between WordSketchApiServer and SketchHandlers pull the score below 75. A codebase at 80+ would have removed deprecated methods once replacements were stabilized and would have extracted model DTOs from algorithm classes.

issues.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser retains ~8 @Deprecated methods alongside the clean replacement API, creating a two-API island in one class
evidence: The file section comments '// ==================== Consolidated clean API ====================' mark the boundary but the deprecated section remains

Deprecated methods are package-private; callers in the same package (BlackLabQueryExecutor) still invoke extractCollocateFromSnippet and extractCollocateFromMatch via extractDependentLemmaFromGroup and executeSurfacePattern
issues.suggestion: Migrate the remaining callers in BlackLabQueryExecutor.extractDependentLemmaFromGroup and executeSurfacePattern to use extractLemmaAt or extractCollocateLemma, then delete the deprecated methods. This removes the dual-API island and reduces ambiguity for future contributors.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer houses 6 inner result DTOs that are imported and consumed by WordSketchApiServer, diverging from the flat-class convention used elsewhere
evidence: WordSketchApiServer.java imports: SemanticFieldExplorer.ComparisonResult, SemanticFieldExplorer.AdjectiveProfile, SemanticFieldExplorer.ExplorationResult, SemanticFieldExplorer.DiscoveredNoun, SemanticFieldExplorer.CoreCollocate, SemanticFieldExplorer.ExploreOptions

All other DTOs in the codebase are top-level classes (QueryResults.WordSketchResult, QueryResults.ConcordanceResult, RadialPlot.Item) — inner-class DTOs in SemanticFieldExplorer are the sole outlier pattern
The SemanticFieldExplorer class Javadoc acknowledges: 'These should be extracted to a dedicated model/ package in a future refactor (see issue 28bc8918 / a430f585)'
issues.suggestion: Create a query/model/ or query/results/ sub-package and extract ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, and ExploreOptions as top-level classes, consistent with QueryResults.*.

issues.summary: HTTP handler logic is split between WordSketchApiServer (public) and SketchHandlers (package-private) without a clear organizing principle, diverging from a single-class-per-concern convention
evidence: WordSketchApiServer contains: handleSketchRequest, handleSurfaceRelations, handleDepRelations, handleFullSketch, handleRelationQuery, handleFullDependencySketch, handleDependencyRelationQuery, handleRelationQueryInternal, handleSemanticFieldExplore, handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti, handleSemanticField, handleSemanticFieldExamples, handleConcordanceExamples, handleVisualRadial, handleBcqlQueryPost

SketchHandlers also contains: handleSketchRequest, handleFullSketch, handleRelationQuery, handleFullDependencySketch, handleDependencyRelationQuery, handleRelationQueryInternal, handleConcordanceExamples, handleVisualRadial, handleBcqlQueryPost
The methods have the same names in both files but SketchHandlers is package-private — no documentation explains which file is authoritative or the refactoring trajectory
issues.suggestion: Document the intended split (e.g., WordSketchApiServer owns server lifecycle and routing; SketchHandlers owns request handling) or complete the extraction and have WordSketchApiServer delegate all handle* methods to SketchHandlers.

root_causes: Incremental refactoring that replaced deprecated methods and extracted handlers was not completed — the old code was left in place alongside the new
SemanticFieldExplorer grew organically with result DTOs added inline rather than upfront design placing them in a model package
